name: No Rest on the Colossus
description:
“Shh quiet, we’re not alone!”

(Artist)

When World War III began, it was unlike the wars that came before. It lasted for two centuries. Neither side dared to make a risky move, and so the world stood breathless as the conflict dragged on—preparing for the worst, hoping for the best.

As the war effort in Europe faltered and naval and aerial transport became increasingly scarce, the Colossus was conceived: a massive rail line stretching from Boston to Lisbon. It remained under construction for nearly the entire duration of the war, an ever-growing monument to desperation and determination.

When it was finally completed, it was a labyrinth—capable of carrying dozens of high-speed trains across the Atlantic. But it would never fulfill its purpose. The nuclear war came first.

Civilization fell, yet the bridge endured. And in the ruin of the old world, survivors of all kinds flocked to it, drawn to the last great relic of a world that no longer existed.

As time passed, the Colossus would slowly began to rot.

I’ve been grinding metro exodus
creator: Goose_Man
